title: Opening stock replay protection removed, enabling duplicate imports
description: Opening stock imports now only dedupe on the idempotency key and openingStockBatchKey. The previous replayProtectionKey derived from the file hash was removed, and the migration drops the replay_protection_key column/index. This means the same file can be imported again under a different batch key without detection, leading to duplicate opening stock postings and materially incorrect inventory/ledger balances.

title: Factory role granted access to financial dispatch endpoints
description: The commit expands the FINANCIAL_DISPATCH authorization expression to include ROLE_FACTORY. The sales dispatch confirmation endpoint is explicitly documented as the “final invoice + AR at shipment” path and is protected by FINANCIAL_DISPATCH, meaning factory users with dispatch.confirm permission can now call it. The DispatchConfirmRequest payload also supports adminOverrideCreditLimit and pricing/tax overrides, which are consumed by SalesCoreEngine to apply dispatch exceptions. This change weakens separation-of-duties by allowing factory operators to perform financial dispatch postings and apply override-driven changes that were previously restricted to admin/accounting roles.

title: Inter-company reconciliation endpoint allows cross-tenant data access
description: The new /api/v1/accounting/reconciliation/inter-company endpoint is guarded only by ROLE_ADMIN/ROLE_ACCOUNTING and accepts companyA/companyB IDs from the caller. The service method loads companies by ID directly and reconciles their ledgers without checking that the requested companies match the current tenant context or that the caller is a super-admin. A tenant user can therefore supply other tenants’ IDs and obtain reconciliation data for companies outside their scope.

title: Inter-company reconciliation allows cross-tenant data exposure
description: The new /reconciliation/inter-company endpoint accepts company IDs from request parameters and calls ReconciliationService.interCompanyReconcile without verifying that the caller is authorized for either company. The service loads Company entities by ID and queries dealer/supplier balances for those companies. Because tenant isolation is enforced by headers/JWT company claims rather than request parameters, an authenticated tenant admin can supply other tenants' company IDs and retrieve reconciliation details (company codes, dealer/supplier IDs, balances), violating tenant boundaries (IDOR). This should be restricted to super-admins or enforce that both companies are within the caller’s allowed tenant set.
